originalTitle: US stocks end mostly lower, dragged by megacap tech; focus on Iran
summary: STORY: Wall Street's main indexes closed mostly lower on Monday, with the exception of the Dow, which gained just under a third of a percent. The S&P 500 shed more than a third of a percent, while the Nasdaq dropped over 1.3%. Shares of Elon Musk's SpaceX weighed heavily on the Nasdaq, plummeting more than 16% for their biggest single-day decline. The rocket and AI company launched its first-ever debt offering on Monday and said it had roughly $100 billion in cash and cash equivalents as of last Friday. SpaceX's stock is still trading above its IPO price of $135. Optimism over AI has supported Wall Street's recent rally, but Melissa Brown, managing director of investment decision research at SimCorp, said the rally could be nearing its peak. "Momentum tends to beget more momentum. So stocks do well, they continue to do well until something happens and they stop doing well. And then typically the market peaks at that point... I do think, though, that this kind of blowout, this huge surge of trading volume does suggest that the peak is closer than farther away." Among other tech names, shares of Alphabet fell 5% following news that Google DeepMind's senior research scientist and Nobel laureate John Jumper said he was leaving for AI startup Anthropic, the lab's latest high-profile exit. Shares of Meta, Amazon and Microsoft also closed lower Monday. The next test for the tech rally will be Micron Technology's quarterly results on Wednesday. Shares of the memory chipmaker are up nearly 300% this year. Among other movers, shares of Apogee Therapeutics soared nearly 47% after AbbVie said it would acquire the biotech company for $10.9 billion in cash. Shares of AbbVie rose more than 6%. Meanwhile, oil prices fell as peace talks between Washington and Tehran continued in Switzerland - though tensions persisted over Lebanon and the Strait of Hormuz.

SpaceX's stock is still trading above its IPO price of $135. Optimism over AI has supported Wall Street's recent rally, but Melissa Brown, managing director of investment decision research at SimCorp, said the rally could be nearing its peak. "Momentum tends to beget more momentum. So stocks do well, they continue to do well until something happens and they stop doing well. And then typically the market peaks at that point... I do think, though, that this kind of blowout, this huge surge of trading volume does suggest that the peak is closer than farther away." Among other tech names, shares of Alphabet fell 5% following news that Google DeepMind's senior research scientist and Nobel laureate John Jumper said he was leaving for AI startup Anthropic, the lab's latest high-profile exit. Shares of Meta, Amazon and Microsoft also closed lower Monday. The next test for the tech rally will be Micron Technology's quarterly results on Wednesday. chineseTitle: 受大型科技股拖累，美国股市收盘大多走低；聚焦伊朗
chineseSummary: 故事：周一，华尔街主要股指收盘大多走低，但道指除外，该指数上涨了不到三分之一。标准普尔 500 指数下跌超过 1/3%，纳斯达克指数下跌超过 1.3%。埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 旗下 SpaceX 的股价对纳斯达克指数造成沉重打击，暴跌超过 16%，创下单日最大跌幅。这家火箭和人工智能公司周一推出了有史以来的首次债券发行，并表示截至上周五，该公司拥有约 1000 亿美元的现金和现金等价物。 SpaceX 的股票交易价格仍高于 135 美元的 IPO 价格。对人工智能的乐观情绪支撑了华尔街近期的涨势，但 SimCorp 投资决策研究董事总经理梅丽莎·布朗 (Melissa Brown) 表示，涨势可能已接近顶峰。 “动能往往会产生更多动能。因此，股票表现良好，它们会继续表现良好，直到发生某些事情，然后不再表现良好。然后通常市场会在那时达到顶峰……不过，我确实认为，这种井喷、交易量的巨大激增确实表明，顶峰已经临近而不是遥远。”在其他科技公司中，Alphabet 的股价下跌了 5%，此前有消息称，谷歌 DeepMind 的高级研究科学家、诺贝尔奖获得者 John Jumper 表示，他将前往人工智能初创公司 Anthropic，这是该实验室最新一次高调退出。 Meta、亚马逊和微软的股价周一也收低。科技股反弹的下一个考验将是美光科技周三公布的季度业绩。这家存储芯片制造商的股价今年上涨了近 300%。在其他推动者中，艾伯维 (AbbVie) 表示将以 109 亿美元现金收购这家生物技术公司后，Apogee Therapeutics 的股价飙升近 47%。艾伯维 (AbbVie) 股价上涨逾 6%。与此同时，随着华盛顿和德黑兰之间的和谈在瑞士继续进行，油价下跌

whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
